what_it_does: This file is a metadata record for a channel attachment: it links the design-assets channel to an image target (Icon_Output) and records when the attachment was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
standouts: The attachment target uses an image:// URI pointing to design-assets/icons/Icon_Output
The record is purely metadata with no embedded image data or description
It is typed as images in the design-assets channel
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Attachment id appears in the filename
Target uses a namespaced URI scheme (image://) for a channel asset
concerns: The image:// URI scheme and its resolution rules are not defined in this file
No image format, dimensions, mime type, or file size are recorded
No author, source message, or provenance context is provided
No alt text, caption, or description for the image
notes: A minimal, timestamped metadata stub that registers an icon image attachment in the design-assets channel.
questions: What does Icon_Output represent and what does it depict?
What image format, resolution, and file size does the target have?
Who added this attachment and in response to which message or design decision?
Is image://design-assets/icons/Icon_Output resolvable and where is the actual file stored?
purpose_vs_actual: The file acts as an attachment metadata record, which matches its content; it does not claim to be more than that.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to register a design asset (an icon image) in a channel memory ledger so it can be referenced, retrieved, and associated with channel activity later.
novelty: The use of an image:// URI target and a metadata-only attachment record without embedded content or descriptive fields.
contribution: This file contributes a machine-readable attachment entry that maps a channel to a specific image asset target and records an addition timestamp, which a fused system could keep as a reference node for design assets.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment metadata file in company/channel-memory that registers an image asset named Icon_Output under the design-assets channel with a 2026 timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to record and make referenceable an icon image attachment in the design-assets channel memory.
